title: ranunculus campanulatus
intro: Ranunculus campanulatus, also known as the tuberous buttercup or yellow water crowfoot, is a perennial aquatic plant native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Ranunculus campanulatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or salve to be used topically or taken internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
